title: Migrate telemetry reader to streaming for large JSONL files

tasks.title: Migrate loadTelemetryRecords to streaming readline implementation
tasks.description:
Rewrite `loadTelemetryRecords` in `src/agent/telemetry/reader.ts` to use `fs.createReadStream` and Node's `readline` module instead of reading the entire file into memory.

Specifics:
- Replace the current `fs.readFile` / `readFileSync` + split implementation with an async generator or a promise-based readline approach
- Use `readline.createInterface({ input: fs.createReadStream(filePath), crlfDelay: Infinity })` to iterate line by line
- Each non-empty line should be JSON-parsed and yielded/collected as before
- Preserve the existing return type of `loadTelemetryRecords` (array of telemetry records) so callers do not need to change their consumption pattern â€” internally stream, but resolve to the same shape
- Handle edge cases: file does not exist (return empty array, same as current behavior), empty file, lines that fail JSON.parse (skip and log to stderr, same as any current error handling)
- The function signature should remain `async` and return `Promise<TelemetryRecord[]>` to keep the change backward-compatible with all callers
- Add or update unit tests in `src/agent/telemetry/reader.test.ts` (or colocated test file) covering: normal multi-record file, empty file, missing file, file with a malformed JSON line interspersed among valid lines

tasks.title: Expose an async-generator streaming API for callers that need incremental processing
tasks.description:
Add a new exported function `streamTelemetryRecords(filePath: string): AsyncGenerator<TelemetryRecord>` (or `AsyncIterable`) in `src/agent/telemetry/reader.ts` that yields records one at a time without buffering the full array.

Specifics:
- Implement as an `async function*` that opens a readline interface over a read stream and yields each parsed record
- This gives future callers (e.g. aggregation commands, cost derivation) a flat-memory path without waiting for all records
- `loadTelemetryRecords` should be refactored to delegate to `streamTelemetryRecords` internally: `const records: TelemetryRecord[] = []; for await (const r of streamTelemetryRecords(path)) records.push(r); return records;`
- Export `streamTelemetryRecords` from `src/agent/telemetry/index.ts` (or wherever the package barrel export lives)
- Add unit tests for `streamTelemetryRecords` covering: yields correct sequence, handles missing file gracefully, skips malformed lines
validationErrors: `loadTelemetryRecords` should be refactored to delegate to `streamTelemetryRecords` internally
Export `streamTelemetryRecords` from the package barrel export (`src/agent/telemetry/index.ts` or equivalent)

tasks.title: Audit and update callers of loadTelemetryRecords
tasks.description:
Search the codebase for all direct callers of `loadTelemetryRecords` and verify they work correctly with the now-async streaming-backed implementation. Migrate any caller that would benefit from the streaming API to use `streamTelemetryRecords` instead.

Specifics:
- Run `grep -r 'loadTelemetryRecords' src/` to find all call sites
- For each caller, confirm it already awaits the result (since the function was already async, this should be a no-op change for most)
- Identify any caller that iterates over all records only to compute an aggregate (e.g. total tokens, cost derivation for `telesis status`) â€” migrate those to use `streamTelemetryRecords` with an accumulator loop to avoid materializing the full array
- Do not change the public contract of commands; only change internal data-access patterns
- Ensure all affected files compile cleanly (`bun run tsc --noEmit` or equivalent)
- Update or add tests for any caller whose internal logic changed
